methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Impulse Labs is a Series A climate & energy company founded by Sam D'Amico that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector private comparables (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M) alongside broader Climate & Energy sector benchmarks that include 8 additional private companies and 1 public reference point.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $25M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next stage, implies a $50M valuation. The peer-median method across the three direct comparables yields a $150M implied value, with the peer cohort ranging from $110M to $200M. These methods reflect material dispersion in how the target's stage and sector positioning map to observable market data.

Triangulating both approaches produces a median implied valuation of $100M, with a low-end estimate of $42.5M and a high-end estimate of $172.5M. The spread reflects the significant variance between stage-based mechanical progression and peer median positioning. Key uncertainties include whether Impulse Labs' operational metrics and market traction align with the higher-valuation peers (Graphyte) or lower-end comparables (Steady Energy), and how capital efficiency and revenue trajectory will influence the next funding round.
